steps.instruction: Add the following components to a 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube at room temperature and mix: ‚ÄĘ 1‚Äď7 őľL entry clone (50‚Äď150 ng) ‚ÄĘ 1 őľL destination vector (150 ng/őľL) ‚ÄĘ TE buffer«į pH 8.0, to 8 őľL

steps.title: Thaw on ice the LR Clonase‚ĄĘ...
steps.instruction: Thaw on ice the LR Clonase‚ĄĘ II enzyme mix for about 2 minutes. Vortex the LR Clonase‚ĄĘ II enzyme mix br¬íefl¬Ę twice (2 seconds each time).

steps.title: To each sample (step 1), add...
steps.instruction: To each sample (step 1), add 2 őľL of LR Clonase‚ĄĘ II enzyme mix to the reaction and mix well by vortexing br¬íefl¬Ę twice. Microcentrifuge br¬íefl¬Ę«Į

steps.title: Return LR Clonase‚ĄĘ II enzyme mix...
steps.instruction: Return LR Clonase‚ĄĘ II enzyme mix to ‚Äď20¬įC or ‚Äď80¬įC storage.

steps.title: Add 1 őľL of the Proteinase...
steps.instruction: Add 1 őľL of the Proteinase K solution to each sample to terminate the reaction. Vortex briefly Incubate samples at 37¬įC for 10 minutes. Transform

steps.title: Transform 1 őľL of each LR...
steps.instruction: Transform 1 őľL of each LR reaction into 50 őľL of One Shot‚ĄĘ OmniMAX‚ĄĘ 2 T1 Phage-Resistant Cells (Cat. no. C8540-03) (5 alpha e. coli). Incubate on ice for 30 minutes. Follow electroboration protocol.

steps.title: T1 Phage-Resistant Cells as described above
steps.instruction: T1 Phage-Resistant Cells as described above. Plate 20 őľL and 100 őľL on LB plates containing 100 őľg/mL ampicillin. Expected results An efficient LR recombination reaction will produce >5000 colonies if the entire LR reaction is transformed and plated.
